text:
Once upon a gentle breeze in a faraway forest, there was a little flower named Lily who felt quite alone and small. She sprang from the ground, her petals as delicate as the softest cotton candy. Her green leaves were like tiny umbrellas, sheltering her from the sun's harsh rays. Every morning, she would stretch her blossoms to the sky, eager for the day to unfold.

One sunny afternoon, while Lily was enjoying the warmth of the sun, a gentle voice whispered through the leaves. It was a butterfly named Flit, who had just landed on a nearby bush. Flit had seen Lily and was drawn to her beauty and resilience. "Hello, little one," Flit said softly. "My name is Flit. I am a butterfly, and I travel far and wide. But you are the most beautiful thing I've seen today."

Lily blushed, her petals tinged with a delicate pink. "Thank you, Flit," she said
